followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I retain the contested verdict and only slightly raise clarity/significance because the other review fairly emphasized the strength of the local-marginal check and the experimental quality. My technical score remains low because the outcome-construction, postselection, and setting-as-preparation issues are still central.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Calling the CHSH claim well supported merely because the visibilities exceed 1/sqrt(2) is too strong; that threshold matters only after the measurement model and sampling assumptions are accepted.

The argument that phase-dependent fourfold coincidences by themselves imply Alice-Bob entanglement is not valid; in this experiment the phases can enter the unnormalized generation/interference amplitude rather than a fixed-state local measurement probability.
The polarizer analogy should not be treated as automatically validating the inferred-outcome protocol for local hidden-variable models.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The other review correctly identifies Eqs. 9-14 as the key methodological bottleneck rather than a cosmetic detail.
The fair-sampling and locality loopholes are real and explicitly acknowledged by the authors.
The local twofold/no-interference result is better supported than I emphasized in cycle 1, especially given Eq. 19.
The supplementary S2 entanglement analysis is crucial and cannot be audited from the supplied bundle.

main_claims.claim: Four-photon frustrated interference produces phase-dependent fourfold coincidences with visibilities high enough that the authors' constructed CHSH expression exceeds 2.
main_claims.notes: The reported S = 2.275 +/- 0.057 and the visibility values are internally consistent with a Bell-type visibility calculation. This support is for the reported constructed statistic, not for the full interpretation as a loophole-free or standard Bell test.
main_claims.paper_location: Results, Figs. 2-3; Eq. 2; table S1 reference

main_claims.claim: The experiment violates a Bell inequality in a way that cannot be described by local realism.
main_claims.notes: The authors acknowledge locality and sampling loopholes, and the outcome construction uses phase-shifted runs. These are not minor presentation issues; they affect whether the CHSH bound of 2 applies to the actual protocol.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Results after Eq. 8 and Fig. 3A; Discussion final paragraph

main_claims.claim: The violation arises from indistinguishability by path identity rather than entanglement.
main_claims.notes: The paper gives plausible reasons that ordinary pairwise polarization, momentum, and frequency entanglement are not the relevant resource. But the broader claim that no operationally relevant entanglement is present depends on supplementary analysis not included in the bundle and on a careful choice of system and bipartition.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Introduction final paragraph; Results paragraph on fibers/filters; Discussion; supplementary S2 reference

main_claims.claim: Local twofold coincidences at Alice and Bob show no interference, so the observed phase dependence is not a local-count modulation.
main_claims.notes: This point is strengthened by the full squeezed-state calculation around Eq. 19. It is a useful no-signaling-style check, though it does not by itself validate the Bell inference.
main_claims.paper_location: Results, Fig. 3B; Materials and Methods Eq. 19

main_claims.claim: The present implementation is not spacelike separated but the architecture could in principle be adapted toward spacelike-separated settings.
main_claims.notes: The paper is appropriately explicit that locality remains open in the reported experiment. The in-principle claim is plausible but not demonstrated here.

missing_assumptions.assumption: For each hidden-variable value, the phase-shifted +1 measurement is equivalent to the missing -1 outcome at the original setting, not merely complementary in averaged quantum coincidence rates.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: CHSH constrains joint distributions for fixed local settings. Replacing missing outcomes with data from other settings can invalidate the classical bound unless this equivalence is justified at the operational hidden-variable level.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The source or selected ensemble is independent of alpha and beta despite the interferometer phases participating in creation/interference.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Fig. 1; Discussion description of manipulating the state during creation
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If the settings affect generation probabilities or hidden-variable distributions, an apparent CHSH violation can arise without contradicting local realism.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Fourfold postselection is fair and setting independent.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Results probability construction; Discussion final paragraph
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Setting-dependent coincidence selection is a standard route for local models to exceed the CHSH bound on the detected subset.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The relevant 'unentangled' system includes all optical modes, source-origin coherences, vacuum sectors, and higher-order SPDC terms, or else the paper clearly limits the claim to a narrower postselected subspace.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Abstract; Results paragraph on destroyed entanglement; Materials and Methods Eq. 15
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Showing individual generated pairs are product states is weaker than showing the complete operational resource used in the Bell analysis is separable.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Phase drift, accidental counts, unequal losses, and detector efficiencies do not materially affect the phase-shifted normalization used to compute S.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Fig. 2; Fig. 3; Eq. 3; table S1 reference
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The inferred four-outcome probabilities are assembled from separate measurements, so stability and calibration assumptions directly enter the reported violation.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the supplementary material prove a CHSH inequality with bound 2 for exactly this inferred-outcome, fourfold-postselected protocol under local hidden-variable models?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The main text references supplementary comparisons but does not include the formal theorem or assumptions needed to audit the bound.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Can a local hidden-variable or classical-wave model exploiting setting-dependent generation, detection, or postselection reproduce Eqs. 2-8 and S > 2?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires expert construction or exclusion of tailored models for the specific interferometer, beyond what is in the bundle.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: What is the entanglement content of the full squeezed-state output, including vacuum and higher SPDC orders, for the relevant Alice/Bob bipartition and for the postselected event class?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The main text only summarizes the entanglement argument and does not provide enough mathematical detail to verify the measure, partition, or bound.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the released raw data and analysis propagate systematics from drift, accidentals, unequal efficiencies, and phase calibration into the quoted S = 2.275 +/- 0.057?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The bundle reports figures and a data link but not the complete analysis workflow or supplementary table values.

novelty_assessment: The experiment is genuinely novel and significant as a multiphoton path-identity/frustrated-interference demonstration with Bell-type correlations. I give the novelty credit for the optical effect and for pushing prior FI work to a statistically significant CHSH-style number. I do not treat the title-level interpretation, 'Bell inequality with unentangled photons,' as established in the main text; it remains an assumption-dependent and likely community-contested reading rather than a clean Bell theorem implementation.
score_rationale: The optical experiment is interesting, likely carefully executed, and novel enough to merit attention. The score is limited because the central interpretive claims require assumptions that are not established in the main text: inferred -1 outcomes, fair fourfold postselection, setting-independent source ensembles, and a full accounting of entanglement. I therefore view the work as a strong FI experiment but a contested Bell/nonlocality claim.
summary: The paper reports a real and interesting four-photon frustrated-interference experiment and, using its own inferred-outcome CHSH construction, obtains S = 2.275 +/- 0.057. I remain unconvinced that the main-text argument establishes a standard Bell violation by unentangled photons: the -1 outcomes are reconstructed from different phase settings, the experiment postselects fourfold coincidences, and the phase settings appear to participate in the interferometric generation process rather than only in measurements on a common source ensemble. The result is promising as path-identity interference, but contested as evidence against local realism without entanglement.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper states that the experiment has no -1 output and defines N(+1,-1|alpha,beta), N(-1,+1|alpha,beta), and N(-1,-1|alpha,beta) using N(+1,+1) at beta+pi, alpha+pi, or both. This requires an additional complementarity assumption beyond the raw coincidence data.
technical_objections.objection: The CHSH probabilities are not obtained from a complete dichotomic measurement at fixed settings; the inaccessible -1 outcomes are inferred from separate +1 coincidence measurements at phase-shifted settings.
technical_objections.paper_location: Results Eqs. 3-7; Materials and Methods Eqs. 9-14

technical_objections.evidence: The authors explicitly say the setup actively manipulates the state during its creation. If alpha and beta affect the generation amplitude or selected source ensemble, the usual CHSH derivation does not apply without an additional measurement-independence/source-independence argument.
technical_objections.objection: The phase settings are not cleanly established as local measurement settings on a common, setting-independent source ensemble.
technical_objections.paper_location: Fig. 1; Results setup description; Discussion paragraph beginning 'Conceptually, our experiment differs'

technical_objections.evidence: The paper postselects simultaneous detections in modes a1, a2, b1, and b2, and explicitly acknowledges the sampling loophole. The detected fourfold sample need not represent all emitted events under local realism.
technical_objections.objection: Fourfold coincidence postselection leaves the fair-sampling/sampling loophole open and may allow local models with setting-dependent detection or coincidence selection.
technical_objections.paper_location: Results construction of probabilities; Discussion final paragraph

technical_objections.evidence: The text addresses pairwise internal degrees of freedom and cites supplementary S2 for a quantitative entanglement bound, but the bundle does not provide enough detail to audit the full multimode, vacuum, higher-order, and postselected-state analysis.
technical_objections.objection: The claim that entanglement is absent 'anywhere within the experimental setup' is stronger than what the provided main text demonstrates.
technical_objections.paper_location: Abstract; Introduction final paragraph; Results paragraph after Eq. 8; Discussion

technical_objections.evidence: In a polarizer experiment, the orthogonal output can be tied to a specific projective measurement model. Here the relabeled outcomes come from different phase settings and times, so a local hidden-variable model need not obey the same alpha-to-alpha+pi relation unless it is imposed as an assumption.
technical_objections.objection: The analogy to single-channel polarizer Bell experiments is not sufficient by itself to justify the classical CHSH bound for this interferometric protocol.
technical_objections.paper_location: Results paragraph after Eqs. 3-6; Materials and Methods, Definition of probabilities

technical_objections.evidence: The normalization combines four rates measured at different phase settings. Drift, unequal coupling, detector efficiencies, accidental subtraction, and phase-setting calibration could bias S, especially with a reported margin of about 4.8 standard deviations.
technical_objections.objection: The statistical error on S appears to account mainly for Poisson counting noise, while systematic effects in the constructed probabilities are not quantified in the main text.
technical_objections.paper_location: Fig. 2 caption; Fig. 3A; Eq. 3; table S1 reference

technical_objections.evidence: The authors note that the second-order approximation can naively suggest superluminal signaling and then repair this with the full squeezed-state treatment. This makes the control of higher-order SPDC terms relevant to the central Bell-statistic analysis.
technical_objections.objection: The truncated state description is acknowledged to give misleading local phase dependence, but the range in which the simplified coincidence formulas remain valid is not fully delimited in the main text.
technical_objections.paper_location: Eq. 2; Materials and Methods, 'The output state of the four-photon FI'
